rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (whether the new reduce_scatter hasattr assertions are sufficient verification), so recall is low for both. X finds some plausibly-real gaps (missing docstring, untested cfg_parallel_size=1 path, uncovered dp_metadata line) with concrete file/line asks, but the report is a messy, self-described failed/incomplete run (low confidence, 'retryable' failure, truncated text, evidence drawn from possibly stale htmlcov artifacts it admits are assumptions) that never reaches a verdict. Y is a coherent, well-grounded technical review that correctly verifies the DPMetadata construction, device_communicator plumbing, and cleanup logic, and lands on APPROVE — matching the actual outcome (Gaohan123 LGTM) — with one reasonably concrete nit. Y is more trustworthy and useful overall despite being slightly less exhaustive in raw comment count.
